scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live v3.0 OpenAPI page-state (fetched directly from the __NEXT_DATA__ JSON embedded in every https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/ads-api/reference/v3.0/<operationId> reference page, which also carries the complete 96-operation linksByVersion index): 7 read streams (the 4 wave2 streams retained at parity — note their base_url still pins /ads/v2, a version SUNSET on 2025-08-05 per Spotify's own migration notice, and ad_accounts itself requests a flat /ad_accounts path that does not exist on the real API at any version, which genuinely requires a business_id; both are pre-existing wave2 discrepancies left untouched per the meta-rule against altering already-migrated behavior — see Known limits in docs.md — plus businesses, business_ad_accounts, assets) and 1 write action (update_campaign). Excluded: the entire billing/CAPI/dataset/pixel/mobile-app/business-invitation/business-member surface (business-administration and conversions-API-integration concerns, not ad-object data sync), async/aggregate reporting, audience/estimate/targeting-lookup endpoints, asset upload (binary), and CRUD write endpoints whose real bodies are deeply nested (createCampaign/createAdSet/createAd's targets/frequency_caps/cost_modifiers graphs, updateAdSet/updateAd's equivalent nested shapes) and therefore not expressible as the engine's flat default JSON write body.

excluded.reason: single-ad-account detail; identical record shape to the business_ad_accounts stream's per-item object (both return the same AdAccount schema), already covered by that stream
UpdateAdAccountRequest's real body includes a nested billing_address object and legal/tax identity fields; account-administration mutation, not ad-object data sync
ad-account team-membership roster; access-administration data, not ad-object data sync
grants a user access to an ad account; access-administration mutation, out of scope
revokes a user's access to an ad account; access-administration mutation, out of scope
changes a member's role on an ad account; access-administration mutation, out of scope
reference lookup list of IAB ad categories for targeting, not a syncable business object
single-ad-set detail; identical record shape to the ad_sets list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

UpdateAdSetRequest's real body is entirely nested campaign-graph fields (frequency_caps, cost_modifiers, targets, budget, pacing, slot_positions) with no flat status/name-only shape; not expressible as the engine's flat default JSON write body
CreateAdSetRequest's real body is a deeply nested campaign-graph object (targets, frequency_caps, budget, pacing); not expressible as the engine's flat default JSON write body
single-ad detail; identical record shape to the ads list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
UpdateAdRequest's real body has no status field and its practical fields (assets/catalog/call_to_action/delivery) are all nested objects; not a safe flat write action
CreateAdRequest's real body requires nested assets/creative objects; not expressible as the engine's flat default JSON write body
creates asset metadata as a precursor to a binary upload; the actual asset bytes still require the binary upload endpoints below, so a metadata-only create has no standalone value for this pass
single-asset detail; identical record shape to the assets list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates asset-type-specific nested fields; narrow sub-resource write with no clear flat shape
uploads the asset's binary bytes; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
begins a chunked binary asset upload; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
transfers a chunk of binary asset data; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
completes a chunked binary asset upload; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
custom-audience definitions for targeting; a distinct, lower-priority catalog than this pass's core campaign-hierarchy/business objects
creates a custom audience; no corresponding read stream is covered in this pass
single custom-audience detail; no corresponding list stream is covered in this pass
deletes a custom audience, which would silently narrow targeting on every ad set still referencing it; out of scope for this pass
updates a custom audience's membership/definition; no corresponding read stream is covered in this pass
requests a signed URL for uploading a custom-audience member-list file; binary/file-upload workflow, out of scope
same signed-upload-URL workflow as above, out of scope
lists CAPI datasets eligible for audience-building; conversions-API integration surface, out of scope
billing/invoicing data, not ad-object data sync
billing/invoicing data, not ad-object data sync
creates a new business (top-level account container); account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation
single-business detail; identical record shape to the businesses list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
renames/reconfigures a business; account-structure administration, not a data-sync mutation
business team-membership roster; access-administration data, not ad-object data sync
accepts a business invitation; account/access-administration mutation, out of scope
single-member detail; access-administration data, out of scope
revokes a user's access to a business; access-administration mutation, out of scope
updates a business member's details; access-administration mutation, out of scope
changes a member's role on a business; access-administration mutation, out of scope
lists ad accounts a specific business member can access; access-administration data, not ad-object data sync
pending business-invitation roster; access-administration data, out of scope
invites a new business member; access-administration mutation, out of scope
cancels a pending business invitation; access-administration mutation, out of scope
provisions a brand-new ad account under a business; account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation
single-campaign detail; identical record shape to the campaigns list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

CreateCampaignRequest requires a nested budget/objective/buying-type graph beyond the flat name/purchase_order/status fields update_campaign covers; creating new campaigns is also a materially different (and riskier, spend-initiating) action than mutating an existing one's lifecycle status
returns a projected reach estimate for a hypothetical targeting configuration; a calculation, not syncable stored data
returns a suggested bid estimate for a hypothetical ad-set configuration; a calculation, not syncable stored data
mobile-app-tracking integration catalog (for in-app-event conversions); a distinct integration surface from this pass's core ad-hierarchy objects
registers a mobile app for conversions tracking; integration-provisioning mutation, out of scope
same mobile-app-tracking integration surface, out of scope
same mobile-app-tracking integration surface, out of scope

shares a mobile app with an ad account; integration-provisioning mutation, out of scope
unshares a mobile app from an ad account; integration-provisioning mutation, out of scope

registers a new conversion pixel; integration-provisioning mutation, out of scope
same pixel-integration surface, out of scope
same pixel-integration surface, out of scope

same CAPI integration surface, out of scope
same CAPI integration surface, out of scope
lists CAPI auth tokens; credential-management surface, out of scope
mints a new CAPI auth token; credential-provisioning action, out of scope
revokes a CAPI auth token, potentially breaking a live server-side conversions integration; credential lifecycle management, out of scope
CAPI dataset catalog; conversions-API integration surface, out of scope
creates a new CAPI dataset; integration-provisioning mutation, out of scope
same CAPI dataset surface, out of scope
same CAPI dataset surface, out of scope
removes an integration from a CAPI dataset; integration-administration mutation, out of scope
same CAPI dataset surface, out of scope
returns CAPI event-matching diagnostic health data, not a syncable business object

requires a mandatory market query parameter with no default and is a search/lookup endpoint (matches by id or free-text query), not a listable catalog with a stable full-sync shape
returns computed reserved-pricing quotes for a hypothetical targeting/budget configuration; a calculation, not syncable stored data

aggregated performance-metrics reporting, out of scope for this pass
audience-insight reporting, out of scope for this pass
kicks off an asynchronous report-generation job; the engine's declarative read/write dialect has no async-job-polling mechanism (StreamHook territory), out of scope for this pass
polls an async report job's status/result; same async-polling limitation as the create endpoint above
